g7_moat.description: Aerospace component manufacturer with FAA certifications, 5-10 year qualification cycles. Switching cost is time (years to qualify new supplier) not money.

quality_multiple_note.summary: Precision Castparts was a real moat business, and part of its low yield reflected genuine aerospace qualification barriers and switching costs.
quality_multiple_note.reason_for_low_yield: The market was paying for quality, but in this case the multiple still required too much optimism about aerospace-cycle durability and future growth.
quality_multiple_note.decision_impact: The quality note is important, but it does not overturn PASS. PCC remains the model case for excellent business, bad price.

management_veto.summary: There was no management veto. The rejection remains a valuation call, not a stewardship or integrity concern.

key_insight: Gate ⑥ is the price discipline gate. Earnings yield of 4.3% means you need strong growth assumptions to justify the price — PCC is a quality business bought at a bad price. Quality ≠ good investment.
